description: Define a consistent spacing and sizing scale using mathematical ratios so components are visually balanced, grouping is clear, and whitespace reinforces information hierarchy.

rules.start_with_too_much_white_space: Start with too much white space — conservative spacing always looks better than cramped layouts
rules.establish_spacing_and_sizing_system: Establish a spacing and sizing system — use a base unit (4px, 8px) and multiply by scale (1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12...)
rules.dont_have_to_fill_whole_screen: You don't have to fill the whole screen — avoid stretching content to viewport width; use containers
rules.grids_are_overrated: Grids are overrated — align by rhythm and intent, not arbitrary grids
rules.relative_sizing_doesnt_scale: Relative sizing doesn't scale — use absolute units in system, scale responsively in breakpoints
rules.avoid_ambiguous_spacing: Avoid ambiguous spacing — every gap between elements should be intentional and expressed in system

interface_design_begins.result: spacing scale is defined and enforced across all components

given: two elements are adjacent in interface
two_elements_adjacent.result: no ambiguous spacing; every gap is intentional

content_container_sized.result: content is readable and respects typography measure

related_content_grouped.result: visual grouping is clear without explicit borders or backgrounds

fields.name: base_unit
fields.label: Foundation spacing unit in pixels (4px or 8px)

fields.name: container_max_width
fields.label: Maximum content width in pixels (calculated from type measure)

related.reason: Spacing reinforces hierarchy through grouping and visual weight
Type measure determines ideal container width for readability
Spacing scale scales responsively across viewport breakpoints
Spacing system is applied within container layout patterns

goals.description: Define a consistent spacing and sizing scale using mathematical ratios so components are visually balanced, grouping is clear, and whitespace reinforces information hierarchy.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Failed operations divided by total attempts

tradeoffs.reason: UI must be usable by all users including those with disabilities
